title: rubus depressus
intro: Rubus depressus, also known as the dwarf raspberry or creeping raspberry, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Additionally, Rubus depressus has been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dyspepsia, and even as a treatment for certain types of cancer.
